data.title: Elon Musk's Lawsuit Against OpenAI and Microsoft Heads to Trial
data.description: Elon Musk's $134 billion lawsuit against OpenAI, Sam Altman, and Microsoft is scheduled for trial on Monday in federal court in Oakland, California. According to NS3.AI, Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers has reduced the case from 26 claims to two counts: unjust enrichment and breach of charitable trust. This decision follows Musk's withdrawal of fraud claims just days before jury selection.

data.title: Trump spoke with the British Prime Minister, emphasizing the need to restore shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: On April 26 local time, US President Trump and British Prime Minister Starmer spoke by phone to discuss the current situation in the Middle East, with both emphasizing the "urgent need to restore shipping in the Strait of Hormuz." The UK stated that the weeks-long blockade has left a large number of sailors stranded in the Gulf region. Starmer warned that this would have "serious consequences" for the global economy and the cost of living for the British public. Iran warned on the same day that the waterway "will not be restored to its previous state under any circumstances." (CCTV)

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Launches Asia's First Offline Community Center in Hong Kong
data.description: ETH Hong Kong Hub, Asia's first offline Ethereum community center, officially opened on April 21, 2026. According to NS3.AI, the opening event attracted over 1,500 registrants, marking a significant milestone for the Ethereum community in the region. The center is supported by the Ethereum Foundation's Ethereum Everywhere program and features an initial cohort of over 30 projects from the Ethereum ecosystem. This initiative aims to foster collaboration and innovation within the Ethereum community across Asia.

data.title: Suspected insider trader went long on LDO, previously profited $2.27 million from trading APE within a single day.
data.description: According to Lookonchain's monitoring, suspected insider traders used two wallets to open long positions in 5.57 million LDO tokens with 5x leverage, worth $5.16 million. Prior to this, they had profited $2.27 million from long positions in APE within a single day.

data.title: Bitcoin Funding Rates Show Long Bias Amid Market Shifts
data.description: According to data from Glassnode, Hyperliquid whales transitioned from a net short to a net long position in early March. According to NS3.AI, Coinglass reported that the seven-day bitcoin perpetual funding rate stands at -0.13%. This negative funding rate has persisted for approximately 47 consecutive days, marking the strongest long bias observed in the dataset.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 78,000 USDT with a 0.53% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 26, 2026, 14:20 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 78,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 78,077.65625 USDT, with a narrowed 0.53% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Expert Warns Freezing Dormant Bitcoin Could Impact Market Pricing
data.description: Samuel "Chad" Patt has suggested that freezing approximately 5.6 million dormant bitcoins could lead to an immediate market repricing. According to NS3.AI, this is because the market would perceive circulating bitcoins as conditionally owned. This discussion arises from BIP-361, a proposal by Jameson Lopp and other developers, which considers phasing out bitcoin’s current cryptographic signatures. The proposal could potentially freeze assets that do not transition to the new system.

data.title: Polymarket Study Reveals Price Discovery Dominated by Minority of Traders
data.description: A recent working paper analyzing Polymarket trades from 2023 to 2025 has revealed that a small fraction of traders are responsible for the majority of price discovery. According to NS3.AI, the study examined data from 1.72 million accounts and a trading volume of $13.76 billion. It found that only 3% of traders significantly influence price movements, while the remaining 97% primarily provide liquidity and tend to incur losses overall. Additionally, trades based on non-public information have a more pronounced impact on prices per dollar traded.

data.title: Whale Transfers Bitcoin to CEX After Two-Year Dormancy
data.description: On April 26, a significant Bitcoin transaction was observed as a whale moved 300 BTC into a centralized exchange (CEX) after a two-year hiatus. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the whale had originally withdrawn these 300 BTC from another CEX three years ago when the price of Bitcoin was $19,329. This transaction has resulted in a profit of $17.6 million for the whale.

data.title: A whale that had been dormant for two years deposited 300 BTC into Binance and will make a profit of $17.6 million if it sells them.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, a whale that had been dormant for two years deposited 300 BTC, worth $23.4 million, into Binance. These 300 BTC were withdrawn from Bitfinex three years ago when the price of BTC was $19,329. The whale has now made a profit of $17.6 million.

data.title: Iran Requires Fees for Ships Passing Through Hormuz Strait
data.description: On April 26, the Chairman of the National Security and Foreign Policy Committee of Iran's Islamic Parliament, Aziz, stated in an interview that all ships wishing to pass through the Strait of Hormuz must pay a fee. According to Odaily, he emphasized that the Strait of Hormuz has become a significant pillar of Iran's strength.

data.title: Michael Saylor Shares Bitcoin Tracker Update
data.description: Michael Saylor, founder of Strategy, has released new information regarding the Bitcoin Tracker, accompanied by the caption 'The ₿eat Goes On.' According to Odaily, Strategy typically announces additional Bitcoin acquisitions the day after such updates are shared.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister departs Muscat, Oman for Pakistan
data.description: On April 26 local time, Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi left Muscat, the capital of Oman, for Islamabad, Pakistan, after meeting with the Sultan of Oman. (CCTV)

data.title: Scallop Identifies Vulnerability in sSUI Reward Pool Contract
data.description: Scallop, a lending protocol within the Sui ecosystem, has reported a vulnerability in an affiliate contract related to its sSUI reward pool, resulting in a loss of approximately 150,000 SUI. According to ChainCatcher, the affected contract has been frozen to prevent further issues. Scallop assures that its core contracts remain secure and that only the sSUI reward pool was impacted, with all other reward pools remaining unaffected. The company has committed to covering 100% of the losses and will provide further updates soon.

data.title: Hyperliquid Platform Whale Positions Reach $3.51 Billion
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass reveals that a whale on the Hyperliquid platform currently holds positions worth $3.51 billion. The long positions amount to $1.77 billion, representing 50.56% of the total, while short positions are valued at $1.73 billion, accounting for 49.44%. The long positions have incurred a loss of $8.52 million, whereas the short positions have a loss of $21.55 million.

Notably, a whale address, 0x0ddf..02, has taken a 3x leveraged short position on Bitcoin at a price of $67,992.10, resulting in an unrealized loss of $9.81 million.

data.title: Iranian official: Ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz must pay a fee.
data.description: On April 26 local time, Aziz, chairman of the National Security and Foreign Policy Committee of the Iranian Islamic Parliament, stated in an interview that all ships wishing to pass through the Strait of Hormuz must pay a fee. He also stated that the Strait of Hormuz has become one of the important pillars of Iran's power. (CCTV)

data.title: DoorDash Collaborates with Tempo for Stablecoin Payouts
data.description: DoorDash is partnering with Stripe-backed Tempo to integrate stablecoin-powered payouts into its marketplace, spanning over 40 countries. According to NS3.AI, this collaboration involves DoorDash, Stripe, Coastal Bank, and ARQ, aiming to introduce stablecoin payments. Andy Fang highlighted that the primary attraction for DoorDash is the faster and more affordable payout system offered by stablecoins.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Endless Toil Plugin Adds Sound Effects to Code Reading
data.description: Andrew Vos has introduced a new GitHub plugin named Endless Toil, which enhances the coding experience by adding sound effects as AI models like Claude or Codex read code. According to NS3.AI, the plugin features three distinct sound levels: groan, wail, and abyss, providing an auditory dimension to the coding process.

data.title: Kevin Warsh's Confirmation Hearing Approaches Amid Crypto Investment Disclosures
data.description: Jeanine Pirro announced the closure of the criminal investigation into Jerome Powell, coinciding with Kevin Warsh's upcoming confirmation hearing. According to NS3.AI, Warsh recently stated that Bitcoin and cryptocurrencies are already integrated into U.S. financial services. His financial disclosures revealed several investments related to the crypto sector.

data.title: White House Urged to Expedite Construction of Secure Banquet Hall
data.description: On April 26, Jin10 reported that recent events have underscored the necessity for a large, secure, and reliable banquet hall within the White House grounds, a feature requested by every president over the past 150 years for various reasons. If the military-grade banquet hall currently under construction at the White House had been completed, such incidents would not occur. The hall must be completed swiftly, as it is not only aesthetically pleasing but also equipped with top-level security measures, without rooms accessible to unauthorized personnel. It is located within the walls of the world's most secure building—the White House. A lawsuit filed by a 'dog-walking woman,' who lacks the standing to bring such a case, must be dismissed immediately. Nothing should interfere with the construction process—the project is not only within budget but also ahead of schedule.
